input.headline: Instagram to start closing Australian teen accounts ahead of social media ban

raw.headline: Instagram to start closing Australian teen accounts ahead of social media ban

winner: Instagram will now close teen accounts in Australia—apparently they're tired of kids posting selfies and not enough pictures of their pet llamas.

candidates.text: Why did Instagram stop trying to post photos in Australia? It knew it was getting "de-grammed"!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Pun on 'de-grammed' is clever and specific to the headline, with good surprise factor and brevity."}

candidates.text: Instagram’s new policy is simple: if you’re under 18 and in Australia, your account gets closed. So teens are already practicing their most important skill—how to pretend they’re fine with being erased from the internet forever.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist on social media policy with a specific reference to Australian teens, concise and witty delivery."}

candidates.text: Instagram is really putting the "gram" in "programmatic deletion.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Puns on 'gram' and 'programmatic deletion' are clever and specific, aligning well with the headline's context."}

candidates.text: Instagram is banning Australian teens to save them from social media, which is a bold move considering the teens were the ones trying to ban the app to save themselves from the internet.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "The joke is concise, surprising, and specific, with a clever twist that plays on the irony of the situation."}

candidates.text: I'm opening my very first Instagram account... I just got a call from my grandma asking if she can post her bingo wins on there too.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat related to the headline but lacks direct connection to the social media ban or account closing. It's mildly funny but feels a bit generic and not tightly tied to the specific situation described in the headline."}

candidates.text: Instagram will now close teen accounts in Australia—apparently they're tired of kids posting selfies and not enough pictures of their pet llamas.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat surprising and specific, but the reference to llamas feels a bit forced and generic. It doesn't fully align with the context of social media bans or teen behavior on Instagram."}

candidates.text: Instagram is currently moving all Australian teens into a giant, physical warehouse where they are forced to sit in silence and look at nothing.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat surprising and specific, but the execution feels a bit forced and overly dark for a humor context, reducing its overall funniness and naturalness."}

candidates.text: Instagram is cracking down on Aussie teens — looks like they're finally taking a break from their usual drama.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat relevant and uses a play on 'drama,' but it's generic and lacks the specificity and surprise needed for maximum humor."}

candidates.text: Instagram will now close Australian teen accounts because they're too young to understand that likes don't count as real friends.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat specific and has a mild surprise element, but the humor feels a bit generic and relies on a common stereotype about social media and teens."}
